tossups.question: MCR’s redevelopment of this building was made more expensive because all its 486 nontempered glass panels had different shapes. A five-level system of traffic loops was added in a failed attempt to deal with traffic at this building. This building now has a dedicated room for playing Twister and a cocktail lounge in a “Connie.” This building was once connected to I. M. Pei’s Sundrome. An Aston Martin DB4 shuttles travelers to a hotel repurposed from this building designed by Lubrano Ciavarra. Writer Vanessa R. Schwartz argues this building was inspired by the concrete shell at Disney's Tomorrowland. Despite having landmark status, in 2001 the (*) Port Authority got permission to dismantle two satellite pods of this building. The head house from this building now makes up part JetBlue’s T5. For 10 points, name this Eero Saarinen designed building at JFK airport.
tossups.answer: TWA Terminal [or TWA Flight Center; or TWA Hotel; or Trans World Flight Center; accept TWA head house; antiprompt on Terminal 5 at JFK Airport before mentioned; prompt on JFK airport or John F. Kennedy International Airport]
tossups.answer_sanitized: TWA Terminal [or TWA Flight Center; or TWA Hotel; or Trans World Flight Center; accept TWA head house; antiprompt on Terminal 5 at JFK Airport before mentioned; prompt on JFK airport or John F. Kennedy International Airport]

tossups.question: An art exhibition at this location was shut down after a rowdy opening night party led to guests breaking a glass door, vomiting on the floor, and graffiti-ing the walls. Vanessa Beecroft’s work “VB54,” which was intended to feature naked women in black body paint with shackles around their ankles, had earlier been pulled from that exhibition. Additions to this structure include a 1969 addition by Roche-Dinkeloo and a 2008 expansion by Gensler. The original portion of this structure has been (*) unused since 2001, and will be converted into a hotel by 2018. This structure was built in 1962 with tube-shaped corridors covered in red carpet, and a thin-shell exterior shaped like a bird. For 10 points, name this terminal at JFK International Airport designed by Eero Saarinen.
tossups.answer: TWA Flight Center [or JetBlue Terminal 5; or T5; or Trans World Flight Center; prompt on JFK International Airport]
tossups.answer_sanitized: TWA Flight Center [or JetBlue Terminal 5; or T5; or Trans World Flight Center; prompt on JFK International Airport]

bonuses.leadin: When he received the 280 million dollar budget for designing this location, Minoru Yamasaki thought there was an extra zero. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: When he received the 280 million dollar budget for designing this location, Minoru Yamasaki thought there was an extra zero. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this complex that included the tallest building in the world when it opened in 1972. Daniel Libeskind designed the rebuilding of this complex after the September 11th attacks.
Yamasaki designed the Pruitt-Igoe housing development in this city. In this city, Dankmar Adler co-designed a red-brick building that is considered one of the first skyscrapers.
Yamasaki also designed one of these buildings in St. Louis, whose concrete vaults may have inspired a later architect’s thin shell roof design for one of these buildings in New York City.
parts_sanitized: Name this complex that included the tallest building in the world when it opened in 1972. Daniel Libeskind designed the rebuilding of this complex after the September 11th attacks.
Yamasaki designed the Pruitt-Igoe housing development in this city. In this city, Dankmar Adler co-designed a red-brick building that is considered one of the first skyscrapers.
Yamasaki also designed one of these buildings in St. Louis, whose concrete vaults may have inspired a later architect's thin shell roof design for one of these buildings in New York City.
answers: World Trade Center [or WTC; accept One World Trade Center; prompt on Ground Zero; prompt on the Twin Towers]
St. Louis (The skyscraper is the Wainwright Building, designed with Louis Sullivan.)
airport terminals [accept TWA Flight Center; prompt on Lambert International Airport or JFK International Airport; prompt on airports; prompt on hotels with “what was its original purpose?”]
answers_sanitized: World Trade Center [or WTC; accept One World Trade Center; prompt on Ground Zero; prompt on the Twin Towers]

airport terminals [accept TWA Flight Center; prompt on Lambert International Airport or JFK International Airport; prompt on airports; prompt on hotels with "what was its original purpose?"]

bonuses.leadin: This artist’s best-known work consists of phrases like “your actions are pointless if no one notices,” which were originally printed on sheets of paper and plastered around Manhattan. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this American feminist artist whose Truisms inspired her to project similar phrases on scrolling light pillars on the wall of the New York Public Library.
Holzer resides in Hoosick Falls, New York, the longtime home of this “primitive” artist who was discovered by Louis Caldor in 1938 at age 78.
Holzer participated in the 2004 Terminal 5 exhibition held at this iconic Eero Saarinen-designed standalone terminal at New York’s JFK Airport.
answers_sanitized: Grandma Moses [or Anna Mary Robertson Moses]
TWA Flight Center [or Trans World Flight Center; accept TWA Terminal]
